title: 02177_Diffusion_Models_as_Artists_Are_we_Closing_the_Gap_between_Humans_and_Machines
positive_examples: Diffusion models have significantly improved the quality of machine-generated drawings, providing a better approximation of human drawing ability compared to previous models like VAEs and GANs.
The study suggests that these models can be used in clinical settings for evaluating emotional trauma and developmental disorders, leveraging drawing as a diagnostic tool.
negative_examples: The remaining gap between human and machine-generated drawings may lead to a lack of originality in machine outputs, potentially stifling creativity in artistic fields.
There is a risk that the technology could be misused to create deceptive or misleading content that could be indistinguishable from human-created art.
